title.en: The security architecture overview: RSNA, keys, and 802.1X

blocks.en: A wired LAN borrows its security from the wire: only a device physically plugged in can send or receive. Wi-Fi has no wire — anyone within radio range can listen and transmit — so 802.11 has to rebuild that protection in software. Before any cipher math, it helps to see the shape of the whole thing: who proves their identity, who blocks the door, and what set of services together turn an open-air link into a trustworthy one.
blocks.zh: 有线局域网的安全性来自“线”本身：只有物理插上线的设备才能收发数据。Wi-Fi 没有这根线——任何在无线电覆盖范围内的设备都能监听和发送——所以 802.11 必须用软件把这层保护重建起来。在进入任何密码学计算之前，先看清整体的轮廓会很有帮助：谁来证明自己的身份、谁来把门挡住，以及哪一组服务共同把一条空中链路变成一条可信的链路。

blocks.en: The central concept is the Robust Security Network Association, or RSNA. It is its own kind of association: not just "this station is attached to this AP", but "this station and this AP have established mutual authentication, agreed on keys, and turned on encryption". An RSNA bundles together a defined set of features — authentication mechanisms for stations, key management algorithms, cryptographic key establishment, ciphers like CCMP and GCMP, the fast BSS transition (FT) mechanism, and cryptographic protection for robust Management frames.
blocks.zh: 核心概念是“健壮安全网络关联（Robust Security Network Association，RSNA）”。它本身就是一种关联：不仅仅是“这个站点连上了这个 AP”，而是“这个站点与这个 AP 已完成相互认证、协商好密钥并开启了加密”。RSNA 把一组确定的功能打包在一起——站点的认证机制、密钥管理算法、密钥的密码学建立、CCMP 与 GCMP 这样的加密算法、快速 BSS 切换（FT）机制，以及对健壮管理帧的密码学保护。

blocks.en: An RSNA may lean on two components that live outside 802.11 itself. The first is an IEEE 802.1X port access entity (PAE): an AP always plays the Authenticator (and EAP Authenticator) role, and a non-AP station always plays the Supplicant (and EAP peer) role. The second is an Authentication Server (AS) — typically reached over RADIUS or Diameter — which actually verifies credentials. But these are optional: a station and AP, or two peers in an IBSS, can also authenticate each other using nothing more than a password.
blocks.zh: RSNA 可能依赖两个位于 802.11 之外的组件。第一个是 IEEE 802.1X 端口访问实体（PAE）：AP 始终扮演认证者（Authenticator，以及 EAP 认证者）角色，非 AP 站点始终扮演请求者（Supplicant，以及 EAP 对端）角色。第二个是认证服务器（AS）——通常通过 RADIUS 或 Diameter 接入——它负责真正校验凭据。但这些都是可选的：一个站点与 AP，或 IBSS 中的两个对端，也可以仅凭一个口令就完成相互认证。

blocks.en: Think of the access-control and data-confidentiality services not as one feature but as a toolkit, each piece doing a distinct job. Authentication establishes identity at the link level; deauthentication tears it down (and, since authentication is a prerequisite for association, also disassociates the station). Data confidentiality encrypts frame contents. Key management produces and refreshes fresh keys via handshakes. Data-origin authenticity proves which station really sent a frame; replay detection rejects unauthorized retransmissions. Fast BSS transition pre-stages security before a handoff. Management frame protection guards robust Management frames. And MAC privacy enhancements let a station rotate its MAC address and reset counters so it cannot be silently tracked.
blocks.zh: 不要把访问控制与数据机密性服务看成单一功能，而要把它当成一个工具箱，每个部件各司其职。认证在链路层确立身份；解认证（deauthentication）将其拆除（并且由于认证是关联的前提，这同时会使站点解除关联）。数据机密性对帧内容加密。密钥管理通过各类握手生成并刷新新鲜的密钥。数据源真实性证明某帧确实由哪个站点发出；重放检测拒绝未经授权的重传。快速 BSS 切换在切换前预先准备好安全上下文。管理帧保护守护健壮管理帧。而 MAC 隐私增强让站点能轮换其 MAC 地址并重置计数器，使其无法被悄悄追踪。

caption.en: The RSNA cast: a Supplicant on the station, an Authenticator on the AP, and an optional Authentication Server reached over a secure channel. The 802.1X Controlled Port stays blocked to general data traffic until authentication and the 4-way handshake finish.
caption.zh: RSNA 中的角色：站点上的请求者、AP 上的认证者，以及通过安全通道接入的可选认证服务器。在认证与 4 次握手完成之前，802.1X 受控端口对普通数据流量始终保持阻断。

blocks.en: Clause 4.10 sketches how 802.1X actually drives this. The trick is the Controlled / Uncontrolled Port model: EAPOL authentication PDUs flow through the Uncontrolled Port, while the Controlled Port — the one that carries your real data — stays blocked until authentication succeeds. There are several recipes, called AKM (Authentication and Key Management) operations. With an AS, the Supplicant and AS run EAP to generate a Pairwise Master Key (PMK), which the AS hands to the Authenticator; with a password or PSK, the station runs SAE to reach the same PMK; FILS authentication compresses the whole thing into four frames; and Opportunistic Wireless Encryption uses a Diffie-Hellman exchange for unauthenticated-but-encrypted links. In every case the PMK then feeds a 4-way handshake that installs the live keys.
blocks.zh: 第 4.10 节勾勒了 802.1X 实际如何驱动这一切。其关键在于“受控/非受控端口”模型：EAPOL 认证 PDU 通过非受控端口流动，而承载你真实数据的受控端口则一直保持阻断，直到认证成功为止。这里有几种“配方”，称为 AKM（认证与密钥管理）操作。使用 AS 时，请求者与 AS 运行 EAP 生成成对主密钥（PMK），由 AS 交给认证者；使用口令或 PSK 时，站点运行 SAE 得到同样的 PMK；FILS 认证把整个过程压缩到四个帧之内；机会式无线加密（OWE）则用 Diffie-Hellman 交换实现“不认证但加密”的链路。在任何一种情形下，PMK 随后都会喂入一次 4 次握手，由握手安装真正使用的密钥。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
    A[Discover AP security policy] --> B{AKM choice}
    B -->|802.1X + AS| C[EAP via Uncontrolled Port -> PMK from AS]
    B -->|password / PSK| D[SAE -> PMK]
    B -->|FILS| E[4 frames: auth + assoc + key confirm]
    B -->|OWE| F[Diffie-Hellman -> PMK]
    C --> G[4-way handshake installs PTK + GTK]
    D --> G
    F --> G
    G --> H[Controlled Port unblocked: encrypted data flows]
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
    A[发现 AP 安全策略] --> B{AKM 选择}
    B -->|802.1X + AS| C[经非受控端口跑 EAP -> 从 AS 取得 PMK]
    B -->|口令 / PSK| D[SAE -> PMK]
    B -->|FILS| E[四个帧: 认证 + 关联 + 密钥确认]
    B -->|OWE| F[Diffie-Hellman -> PMK]
    C --> G[4 次握手安装 PTK + GTK]
    D --> G
    F --> G
    G --> H[受控端口解锁: 加密数据开始流动]

blocks.en: Infrastructure isn't the only shape. In an IBSS (ad-hoc) or PBSS, there's no single AP, so every station carries both Supplicant and Authenticator roles. That means peers run handshakes in both directions: N supplicants need N(N-1) 4-way handshakes, and a secure link only exists once both directions complete. PMKSA caching lets a returning station name a previously established key so it can skip the slow part next time.
blocks.zh: 基础设施模式并非唯一形态。在 IBSS（自组织）或 PBSS 中没有单一 AP，因此每个站点同时承担请求者与认证者两种角色。这意味着对端要双向运行握手：N 个请求者需要 N(N-1) 次 4 次握手，且只有当双向都完成后才存在安全链路。PMKSA 缓存让回访的站点能指名一个先前已建立的密钥，从而在下次跳过最慢的那一步。

summary.en: What the 4-way handshake actually accomplishes, and the selector idea
summary.zh: 4 次握手究竟完成了什么，以及“选择子”的概念

blocks.en: Per clause 4.10.3.2, once a PMK exists (from an AS, from SAE, or as a raw PSK), the Authenticator initiates a 4-way handshake of EAPOL-Key PDUs that does a remarkable amount of work in four messages: it confirms a live peer holds a current PMK, derives a fresh Pairwise Transient Key (PTK) from it, installs the pairwise encryption and integrity keys, transports the Group Temporal Key (GTK) and its sequence number from Authenticator to Supplicant, verifies that the negotiated RSN capabilities are valid, and confirms the cipher suite selection. Only on successful completion are the 802.1X Controlled Ports unblocked for general data. The "selector" idea threaded through clause 4 is exactly this negotiation: the AP advertises which AKM and which cipher suites it supports, the station selects from that list, and the handshake confirms the choice — so authentication method, key-management protocol, and cipher are all named and agreed, not assumed. Later, the group key handshake refreshes only the GTK when group keys rotate (4.10.3.2), and group-addressed robust Management frames are protected with BIP (4.10.8).
blocks.zh: 依据第 4.10.3.2 节，一旦存在 PMK（来自 AS、来自 SAE，或本身就是一个 PSK），认证者便发起一次基于 EAPOL-Key PDU 的 4 次握手，在四条消息内完成相当多的工作：确认存活的对端持有一个当前有效的 PMK，从中派生出新鲜的成对临时密钥（PTK），安装成对加密与完整性密钥，将组临时密钥（GTK）及其序号从认证者传给请求者，校验所协商的 RSN 能力是否有效，并确认加密套件的选择。只有在成功完成后，802.1X 受控端口才会对普通数据解锁。贯穿第 4 章的“选择子（selector）”概念正是这一协商：AP 通告自己支持哪些 AKM 与哪些加密套件，站点从该列表中选择，握手再确认这一选择——于是认证方法、密钥管理协议与加密算法都是被指名并达成一致的，而非默认假定。此后，组密钥握手在组密钥轮换时只刷新 GTK（4.10.3.2），而组寻址的健壮管理帧由 BIP 保护（4.10.8）。

4.5.4 Access control and data confidentiality services — authentication, deauthentication, data confidentiality, key management, data origin authenticity, replay detection, FT, management frame protection, MAC privacy enhancements

4.10 IEEE Std 802.11 and IEEE Std 802.1X-2020 — functional model overview; AKM operations (AS, password/PSK, FILS, OWE); IBSS/PBSS models; authenticator-to-AS; PMKSA caching; group-addressed robust management frame protection
